name: Embalse del Rosarito by the Garganta de Santa María — circular from Candela
name_original: Embalse del Rosarito por la Garganta de Santa María — circular desde Candela
region_intro: Jogging around Candeleda offers diverse terrain on the southern slopes of the Sierra de Gredos mountains and within the Tiétar Valley. The region features extensive trail networks through oak and pine forests, alongside gorges, and past natural pools. Runners can explore varied landscapes from riverine paths to dehesa, providing options for different preferences. This area's unique microclimate supports outdoor activities for much of the year. Best jogging routes around Candeleda The most popular jogging route is Embalse del Rosarito by the Garganta de Santa María — circular from Candela, a 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 26 minutes to complete. This route follows the scenic Garganta de Santa María, offering views of the natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local runners is Sauce Spring – Puente del Puerto loop from Candeleda, a difficult 9.6 miles (15.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and leads through varied landscapes of the Sierra de Gredos foothills. Local runners also love the Mirador El Cardenillo loop from Madrigal de la Vera, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through forested areas and offering panoramic viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 13 minutes. Jogging around Candeleda is defined by the Sierra de Gredos mountains, the Tiétar Valley, and numerous gorges and oak forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult running, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Candeleda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Candeleda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Candeleda?
A: Candeleda offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 20 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various preferences, ranging from easy paths to more challenging mountain runs.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Candeleda?
A: Yes, Candeleda has several easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. For instance, the area features paths through the Tiétar Valley and along riverine forests, offering softer terrains. While specific easy routes are not detailed here, the overall network includes options suitable for all levels.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Candeleda?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running paths in Candeleda are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1158210168/embalse-del-rosarito-by-the-garganta-de-santa-maria-circular-from-candela" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Rosarito by the Garganta de Santa María — circular from Candela&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.1-mile (13.0 km) loop.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on Candeleda's running trails?
A: Jogging in Candeleda offers a diverse range of landscapes. You can run through extensive oak and pine forests, along the scenic Garganta de Santa María, and past natural pools. The region is nestled between the dramatic Sierra de Gredos mountains and the fertile Tiétar Valley, providing varied terrains from mountain foothills to riverine paths and dehesa (wooded pastures).

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by natural pools or gorges?
A: Yes, the Candeleda area is known for its beautiful gorges and natural pools, which are often accessible from running routes. Gorges like Santa María, Chilla, and Alardos descend from the Gredos mountains, forming refreshing spots such as "Charco Carreras" and "Charco Palomas." You can find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6472086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7005777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gredos Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some points of interest or landmarks I can see while running in Candeleda?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter several points of interest. Some routes might lead you near the ancient Castro del Raso archaeological site or the Santuario de Nuestra Señora de Chilla. For stunning views, consider routes that offer panoramic viewpoints, such as those from Puerto de Candeleda. You can also find highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2641568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elola Refuge – Laguna Grande de Gredos&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2777151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Pelayos Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is Candeleda suitable for family-friendly jogging?
A: Candeleda offers a variety of trails, including easier paths that can be suitable for families. The diverse terrain means you can find flatter sections through the valley or along riverbanks, which are generally more family-friendly. Always check the route's difficulty and length to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Are the running trails in Candeleda dog-friendly?
A: Many outdoor trails in Candeleda are generally dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy a run with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to check local regulations for specific routes or protected areas. The extensive forests and open dehesa provide ample space for dogs to enjoy.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Candeleda?
A: Candeleda benefits from a unique microclimate, featuring mild winters and warm summers. This makes it suitable for outdoor activities like jogging for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures, while summer mornings or evenings provide ideal conditions to enjoy the shaded forest trails.

Q: Are there running routes with good viewpoints in Candeleda?
A: Yes, Candeleda's location on the southern slopes of the Sierra de Gredos means many routes offer spectacular views. Trails leading to higher elevations or through specific miradores (viewpoints) provide breathtaking rewards. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24532397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador El Cardenillo loop from Madrigal de la Vera&lt;/a&gt; is known for its panoramic vistas.

Q: How do other runners rate the trails in Candeleda?
A: The running routes in Candeleda are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the natural beauty and diverse options available.

Q: Is parking available near the running routes in Candeleda?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Candeleda, especially near popular trailheads or village access points. For specific routes, it's often possible to find roadside parking or designated areas. For longer or more remote trails, planning your starting point and parking in advance is recommended.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Experienced runners will find plenty of challenging options in Candeleda, particularly in the Sierra de Gredos foothills. Routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sauce Spring – Puente del Puerto loop from Candeleda&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 9.6 miles (15.4 km) with significant elevation gain, provide a rigorous workout. The region also hosts the annual "Trail El Guerrero" competition, indicating a network of demanding trails.

Q: Can I find routes that take me through forests?
A: Absolutely. Candeleda is rich in forest trails. You can enjoy runs through extensive oak forests, including Candeleda's largest oak forest, as well as pine forests and gallery forests alongside waterways. These provide excellent shade and a tranquil environment for running, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Candela – Puente Viejo de Candeleda loop from Candeleda&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Candeleda is a charming village in the province of Ávila, in the Sierra de Gredos, known for its natural beauty and rich history. Candeleda is a perfect destination for a relaxing getaway full of outdoor activities.
At the northeastern end of the Rosarito Reservoir, taking this path that takes you to the riverbed of the same swamp, you can see its waters and the area as a whole!

On the south side of the Circo de Gredos there appear a group of streams that join until reaching the town of Candela. At this point the river becomes large and continues until it reaches, following a southerly direction, the Rosarito Reservoir.
